commands.config_desc: Print the effective auto mode config as JSON: your settings where set, defaults otherwise
commands.rewind_desc: Restore the code and/or conversation to a previous point

dialogs.open_in_claude_code_on_the_web: Open in Claude Code on the web

mcp_tools.execute_javascript_code_in_the_context_o: Execute JavaScript code in the context of the current page. The code runs in the page's context and can access DOM elements, cookies, localStorage, etc.
mcp_tools.get_an_accessibility_tree_representation: Get an accessibility tree representation of elements on the page. By default returns all elements in the viewport.
mcp_tools.navigate_to_a_url_or_go_forward_back_in: Navigate to a URL, or go forward/back in browser history. If you don't have a valid tab ID, use tabs_list first.
mcp_tools.left_click_at_the_given_coordinates_the: Left-click at the given coordinates. The frontmost application must be in the session allowlist at the time of the click.
mcp_tools.double_click_at_the_given_coordinates_s: Double-click at the given coordinates. Selects a word in most text editors. The frontmost application must be in the session allowlist.
mcp_tools.triple_click_at_the_given_coordinates_s: Triple-click at the given coordinates. Selects a line in most text editors. The frontmost application must be in the session allowlist.
mcp_tools.right_click_at_the_given_coordinates_op: Right-click at the given coordinates. Opens a context menu in most applications. The frontmost application must be in the session allowlist.
mcp_tools.type_text_into_whatever_currently_has_ke: Type text into whatever currently has keyboard focus. The frontmost application must be in the session allowlist.
mcp_tools.scroll_at_the_given_coordinates_the_fro: Scroll at the given coordinates. The frontmost application must be in the session allowlist at the time of the scroll.
mcp_tools.move_the_mouse_cursor_without_clicking: Move the mouse cursor without clicking. Useful for triggering hover states. The frontmost application must be in the session allowlist.
mcp_tools.read_the_current_clipboard_contents_as_t: Read the current clipboard contents as text. Requires the `clipboardRead` grant.
mcp_tools.write_text_to_the_clipboard_requires_th: Write text to the clipboard. Requires the `clipboardWrite` grant.
mcp_tools.javascript_tool: Execute JavaScript code in the context of the current page. The code runs in the page's context and can interact with the DOM, window object, and page variables. Returns the result of the last expression or any thrown errors. If you don't have a valid tab ID, use tabs_context_mcp first to get available tabs.
mcp_tools.read_page: Get an accessibility tree representation of elements on the page. By default returns all elements including non-visible ones. Output is limited to 50000 characters by default. If the output exceeds this limit, you will receive an error asking you to specify a smaller depth or focus on a specific element using ref_id. Optionally filter for only interactive elements. If you don't have a valid tab ID, use tabs_context_mcp first to get available tabs.
mcp_tools.form_input: Set values in form elements using element reference ID from the read_page tool. If you don't have a valid tab ID, use tabs_context_mcp first to get available tabs.
mcp_tools.navigate: Navigate to a URL, or go forward/back in browser history. If you don't have a valid tab ID, use tabs_context_mcp first to get available tabs.
mcp_tools.resize_window: Resize the current browser window to specified dimensions. Useful for testing responsive designs or setting up specific screen sizes. If you don't have a valid tab ID, use tabs_context_mcp first to get available tabs.
mcp_tools.gif_creator: Manage GIF recording and export for browser automation sessions. Control when to start/stop recording browser actions (clicks, scrolls, navigation), then export as an animated GIF with visual overlays (click indicators, action labels, progress bar, watermark). All operations are scoped to the tab's group. When starting recording, take a screenshot immediately after to capture the initial state as the first frame. When stopping recording, take a screenshot immediately before to capture the final state as the last frame. For export, either provide 'coordinate' to drag/drop upload to a page element, or set 'download: true' to download the GIF.
mcp_tools.upload_image: Upload a previously captured screenshot or user-uploaded image to a file input or drag & drop target. Supports two approaches: (1) ref - for targeting specific elements, especially hidden file inputs, (2) coordinate - for drag & drop to visible locations like Google Docs. Provide either ref or coordinate, not both.
mcp_tools.get_page_text: Extract raw text content from the page, prioritizing article content. Ideal for reading articles, blog posts, or other text-heavy pages. Returns plain text without HTML formatting. If you don't have a valid tab ID, use tabs_context_mcp first to get available tabs.
mcp_tools.read_console_messages: Read browser console messages (console.log, console.error, console.warn, etc.) from a specific tab. Useful for debugging JavaScript errors, viewing application logs, or understanding what's happening in the browser console. Returns console messages from the current domain only. If you don't have a valid tab ID, use tabs_context_mcp first to get available tabs. IMPORTANT: Always provide a pattern to filter messages - without a pattern, you may get too many irrelevant messages.
mcp_tools.read_network_requests: Read HTTP network requests (XHR, Fetch, documents, images, etc.) from a specific tab. Useful for debugging API calls, monitoring network activity, or understanding what requests a page is making. Returns all network requests made by the current page, including cross-origin requests. Requests are automatically cleared when the page navigates to a different domain. If you don't have a valid tab ID, use tabs_context_mcp first to get available tabs.
mcp_tools.switch_browser: Switch which Chrome browser is used for browser automation. Call this when the user wants to connect to a different Chrome browser. Broadcasts a connection request to all Chrome browsers with the extension installed — the user clicks 'Connect' in the desired browser.
mcp_tools.request_access: Request user permission to control a set of applications for this session. Must be called before any other tool in this server. The user sees a single dialog listing all requested apps and either allows the whole set or denies it. Call this again mid-session to add more apps; previously granted apps remain granted. Returns the granted apps, denied apps, and screenshot filtering capability.
mcp_tools.zoom: Take a higher-resolution screenshot of a specific region of the last full-screen screenshot. Use this liberally to inspect small text, button labels, or fine UI details that are hard to read in the downsampled full-screen image. IMPORTANT: Coordinates in subsequent click calls always refer to the full-screen screenshot, never the zoomed image. This tool is read-only for inspecting detail.
mcp_tools.left_click: Left-click at the given coordinates. The frontmost application must be in the session allowlist at the time of this call, or this tool returns an error and does nothing.
mcp_tools.double_click: Double-click at the given coordinates. Selects a word in most text editors. The frontmost application must be in the session allowlist at the time of this call, or this tool returns an error and does nothing.
mcp_tools.triple_click: Triple-click at the given coordinates. Selects a line in most text editors. The frontmost application must be in the session allowlist at the time of this call, or this tool returns an error and does nothing.
mcp_tools.right_click: Right-click at the given coordinates. Opens a context menu in most applications. The frontmost application must be in the session allowlist at the time of this call, or this tool returns an error and does nothing.
mcp_tools.middle_click: Middle-click (scroll-wheel click) at the given coordinates. The frontmost application must be in the session allowlist at the time of this call, or this tool returns an error and does nothing.
mcp_tools.type: Type text into whatever currently has keyboard focus. The frontmost application must be in the session allowlist at the time of this call, or this tool returns an error and does nothing. Newlines are supported. For keyboard shortcuts use `key` instead.
mcp_tools.scroll: Scroll at the given coordinates. The frontmost application must be in the session allowlist at the time of this call, or this tool returns an error and does nothing.
mcp_tools.mouse_move: Move the mouse cursor without clicking. Useful for triggering hover states. The frontmost application must be in the session allowlist at the time of this call, or this tool returns an error and does nothing.
mcp_tools.switch_display: Switch which monitor subsequent screenshots capture. Use this when the application you need is on a different monitor than the one shown. The screenshot tool tells you which monitor it captured and lists
mcp_tools.cursor_position: Get the current mouse cursor position. Returns image-pixel coordinates relative to the most recent screenshot, or logical points if no screenshot has been taken.
mcp_tools.left_mouse_down: Press the left mouse button at the current cursor position and leave it held. The frontmost application must be in the session allowlist at the time of this call, or this tool returns an error and does nothing. Use mouse_move first to position the cursor. Call left_mouse_up to release. Errors if the button is already held.
mcp_tools.left_mouse_up: Release the left mouse button at the current cursor position. The frontmost application must be in the session allowlist at the time of this call, or this tool returns an error and does nothing. Pairs with left_mouse_down. Safe to call even if the button is not currently held.
mcp_tools.teach_step: Show one guided-tour tooltip and wait for the user to click Next. On Next, execute the actions,
mcp_tools.claude_in_chrome: Automates your Chrome browser to interact with web pages - clicking elements, filling forms, capturing screenshots, reading console logs, and navigating sites. Opens pages in new tabs within your existing Chrome session. Requires site-level permissions before executing (configured in the extension).

cli_tools.diagnose_and_verify_your_claude_code_ins: Diagnose and verify your Claude Code installation and settings
cli_tools.get_comments_from_a_github_pull_request: Get comments from a GitHub pull request
cli_tools.complete_a_security_review_of_the_pendin: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
cli_tools.deprecated_use_config_to_change_output: Deprecated: use /config to change output style
cli_tools.alias_definition_in_the_form_name_value: Alias definition in the form name=value
cli_tools.provide_an_explanation_of_a_shell_comman: Provide an explanation of a shell command
cli_tools.visualize_current_context_usage_as_a_col: Visualize current context usage as a colored grid
cli_tools.run_a_command_immune_to_hangups: Run a command immune to hangups
cli_tools.delay_for_a_specified_amount_of_time: Delay for a specified amount of time
cli_tools.run_a_command_on_slurm_cluster_nodes: Run a command on SLURM cluster nodes
cli_tools.run_a_command_with_a_time_limit: Run a command with a time limit
cli_tools.command_to_run_on_the_cluster: Command to run on the cluster
cli_tools.create_type_stub_file_s_for_import: Create type stub file(s) for import
cli_tools.verify_completeness_of_types_in_py_typed: Verify completeness of types in py.typed package
cli_tools.skip_type_analysis_of_unannotated_functi: Skip type analysis of unannotated functions
cli_tools.use_exit_code_of_1_if_warnings_are_repor: Use exit code of 1 if warnings are reported
cli_tools.use_up_to_n_threads_to_parallelize_type: Use up to N threads to parallelize type checking
cli_tools.specify_files_or_directories_to_analyze: Specify files or directories to analyze (overrides config file)
cli_tools._aws_sdk_client_sso: AWS SDK for JavaScript Sso Client for Node.js, Browser and React Native
cli_tools._aws_sdk_client_bedrock: AWS SDK for JavaScript Bedrock Client for Node.js, Browser and React Native
cli_tools._aws_sdk_client_sts: AWS SDK for JavaScript Sts Client for Node.js, Browser and React Native
cli_tools.update_plan: Present a plan to the user for approval before taking actions. The user will see the domains you intend to visit and your approach. Once approved, you can proceed with actions on the approved domains without additional permission prompts.
cli_tools.shortcuts_list: List all available shortcuts and workflows (shortcuts and workflows are interchangeable). Returns shortcuts with their commands, descriptions, and whether they are workflows. Use shortcuts_execute to run a shortcut or workflow.
cli_tools.shortcuts_execute: Execute a shortcut or workflow by running it in a new sidepanel window using the current tab (shortcuts and workflows are interchangeable). Use shortcuts_list first to see available shortcuts. This starts the execution and returns immediately - it does not wait for completion.
cli_tools.sharp: High performance Node.js image processing, the fastest module to resize JPEG, PNG, WebP, GIF, AVIF and TIFF images
cli_tools.left_click_drag: Press, move to target, and release. The frontmost application must be in the session allowlist at the time of this call, or this tool returns an error and does nothing.
cli_tools.open_application: Bring an application to the front, launching it if necessary. The target application must already be in the session allowlist — call request_access first.
cli_tools.list_granted_applications: List the applications currently in the session allowlist, plus the active grant flags and coordinate mode. No side effects.
cli_tools.hold_key: Press and hold a key or key combination for the specified duration, then release. The frontmost application must be in the session allowlist at the time of this call, or this tool returns an error and does nothing. System-level combos require the `systemKeyCombos` grant.
cli_tools.computer_batch: Execute a sequence of actions in ONE tool call. Each individual tool call requires a model→API round trip (seconds);
cli_tools.teach_batch: Queue multiple teach steps in one tool call. Parallels computer_batch:
cli_tools.web_setup: Setup Claude Code on the web (requires connecting your GitHub account)
cli_tools.loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m)
cli_tools.schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule.
cli_tools.claude_api: Build apps with the Claude API or Anthropic SDK.\nTRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`/`claude_agent_sdk`, or user asks to use Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Agent SDK.\nDO NOT TRIGGER when: code imports `openai`/other AI SDK, general programming, or ML/data-science tasks.
cli_tools.duration_sleep: Duration to sleep (seconds or with suffix like 5s, 2m, 1h)
cli_tools.duration_timeout: Duration to wait before timing out (e.g., 10, 5s, 2m)
cli_tools.aws_sdk_for_javascript_bedrock: AWS SDK for JavaScript Bedrock Runtime Client for Node.js, Browser and React Native
cli_tools.aws_sdk_for_javascript_cognito: AWS SDK for JavaScript Cognito Identity Client for Node.js, Browser and React Native
cli_tools.read_file_or_directory_list_fr: Read file or directory list from stdin
